heading: Agricultural and food policy research centers
label: 3155 Agricultural and food policy research centers
notes: Codificationand made identical amendments to this section. The amendments by were repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246

Amendments2018—Subsec. (e). substituted “2023” for “2018”.Pub. L. 115–3342014—, substituted “Agricultural and food policy” for “Policy” in section catchline.Pub. L. 113–79, § 7106(1)Subsec. (a). , substituted “Secretary shall, acting through the Office of the Chief Economist, make competitive grants to, or enter into cooperative agreements with,” for “Secretary may make grants, competitive grants, and special research grants to, and enter into cooperative agreements and other contracting instruments with,” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 113–79, § 7106(2)Subsec. (b). , added subsec. (b) and struck out former subsec. (b). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “State agricultural experiment stations, colleges and universities, other research institutions and organizations (including the Food Agricultural Policy Research Institute, the Agricultural and Food Policy Center, the Rural Policy Research Institute, and the National Drought Mitigation Center), private organizations, corporations, and individuals shall be eligible to apply for funding under subsection (a) of this section.”Pub. L. 113–79, § 7106(3)Subsec. (c). , added subsec. (c). Former subsec. (c) redesignated (d).Pub. L. 113–79, § 7106(5)Subsec. (d). , redesignated subsec. (c) as (d). Former subsec. (d) redesignated (e).Pub. L. 113–79, § 7106(4)Subsec. (d)(2). , inserted “applied” after “theoretical and”.Pub. L. 113–79, § 7106(6)Subsec. (e). , added subsec. (e) and struck out former subsec. (e). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this section for each of fiscal years 1996 through 2012.”Pub. L. 113–79, § 7106(7), redesignated subsec. (d) as (e).Pub. L. 113–79, § 7106(4)2008—Subsec. (a)(1). , inserted “(including commodities, livestock, dairy, and specialty crops)” after “agricultural sectors”.Pub. L. 110–246, § 7111(1)Subsec. (b). , inserted “(including the Food Agricultural Policy Research Institute, the Agricultural and Food Policy Center, the Rural Policy Research Institute, and the National Drought Mitigation Center)” after “research institutions and organizations”.Pub. L. 110–246, § 7111(2)Subsec. (d). , substituted “2012” for “2007”.Pub. L. 110–246, § 7111(3)2002—Subsec. (c)(3). , substituted “collect, analyze, and disseminate data” for “collect and analyze data”.Pub. L. 107–171, § 7216Subsec. (d). , substituted “2007” for “2002”.Pub. L. 107–171, § 71031998—Subsec. (a). , inserted “and trade agreements” after “public policies” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–185, § 224Subsec. (d). , substituted “each of fiscal years 1996 through 2002” for “fiscal years 1996 and 1997”.Pub. L. 105–185, § 301(a)(3)

Effective Date of 2008 AmendmentAmendment of this section and repeal of by effective , the date of enactment of , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4 of Pub. L. 110–246section 8701 of this titleMay 22, 2008

children.text: the farm and agricultural sectors (including commodities, livestock, dairy, and specialty crops);
rural families, households, and economies; and
unbiased, nonpartisan economic analysis to Congress on the areas specified in paragraphs (1) through (4) of subsection (a); or

objective, scientific information to Federal agencies and the public to support and enhance efficient, accurate implementation of Federal drought preparedness and drought response programs, including interagency thresholds used to determine eligibility for mitigation or emergency assistance.

extensive databases, models, and demonstrated experience in providing Congress with agricultural market projections, rural development analysis, agricultural policy analysis, and baseline projections at the farm, multiregional, national, and international levels; or
information, analysis, and research relating to drought mitigation.
quantify the implications of public policies and regulations;
develop theoretical and applied research methods;
collect, analyze, and disseminate data for policymakers, analysts, and individuals; and

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2023.
